paragraphs.body: On the day of my High school graduation, happy but terrified, sad but overly joyed about the 4 years that were coming to an end, there was one more thing I had to conquer to move on, As the Salutatorian, I was obligated to do the one thing that doesn’t bring me comfort, Speak in front of 500-600 people including teacher, classmates, and my family. With little to no experience in public speaking besides a little class presentation, this took the cake for sure. As the graduation ceremony began, the clock in my head started to run, and my heart started to race the closer I came to speak in front of the school. I began to sweat and could quite literally hear my heartbeat out of my chest, and it was so loud that it would distract me from the ceremony and the other speeches. The class president’s speech had come to an end, knowing that I was up next, there was no turning back. Jasmine (class pres.) sat down, and Ms. Agree, the ceremony coordinator, went up to announce my name while she hyped me up, giving me a confidence boost lol. The nerves were still like no other, as I walked up, I did so with my head high to mask the rather incurving fear running through my whole body, I began to speak, thanking all my classmates and teachers as I started to talk you could hear the shivers in my voice the masking of my fear was creeping in slowly until I hear my mom yell “Go, son” which gave me the strength to end the speech with a bang “As you look to your right and your life tell each other you did it you have conquered what most could not and soon after I conqueror my fear of speaking at my graduation. When I got my diploma, it was like I was shot with an arrow that gives you wings, I felt on top of the world, receiving something that wasn’t just for me but for future generations after me. Walking across the stage, hearing my name being shouted by friends, Family, and teacher, there was nothing you could do but smile and almost shed a tear. The goal was reached in front of hundreds of people surrounded by classmates who had just done the same thing it was inspirational, actually; it was pure inspiration. You could feel the joy and feel the energy of that feeling of accomplishing something it was everywhere. While some think it’s just a paper, it also represents what I will become and what I can be, and it is an example of what could be if you put your mind to it. But as high school is just as good to graduate from, the goal is to graduate and earn my diploma at The University of Pittsburgh. All I think about is what it would be like to walk across those stages. Hearing my name being called “,” and all of a sudden, you hear the same people from high school cheering you on to conquer the next part of your journey, sounding like hyenas in the crowd, the celebratory whistles, the loud claps as if you were clapping yourself, I think about what it would feel like to walk across the stages to shake the Dean’s hand and take the diploma with these off and it made me feel invincible like I could achieve anything in the world. What I could be is a blueprint for generations behind me, and what I could do is be someone who brings notoriety to the community that things can be accomplished. I want to spread the message that you can conquer anything you see What I’ve learned is that the word conquers, depending on the person, could be positive or negative. Sir Edmund’s quote, “It is not the mountain we conquer but ourselves,” is something that hit me because it wasn’t the idea of Mount Et he was worried it was his state of mind and being able to push himself to be the first person to reach the top. That’s what I believe my life to be it’s not about where I'm going but whether I am willing to get there without giving up, no matter the obstacles, and no matter the struggle, am I able to reach Mount Everest. Right now, I am getting through the obstacles, but I know there are days ahead when I want to give up. I want to be able to conquer everything that stands in my way. To conquer isn’t to join a gang, but to pave your way, to conquer isn’t to skip school, but to graduate, to conquer isn’t to give up on your dreams but to pursue them no matter the obstacles. Conquering isn’t violence it’s a mindset that shows you can achieve and accomplish things even with ripples in the road. You are a conqueror just by waking up; you could be a conqueror just by finishing an essay for class just like Sir Edmund said, we must conquer ourselves before the mountain, meaning facing our fears and becoming the best versions of ourselves. To conquer can be demonstrated by overcoming obstacles that gave you trouble, to conquer can mean to complete a task you gave you all too. Conquering gives much more than just the feelings of fear and control; it also implies triumph, growth, and perseverance. In which you show such a type of resilience and determination to conquer.

paragraphs.body: We can all achieve big things in this world, even if we think these things are out of reach, some people have done it before us, so we look at those who took the same path so that we have the inspiration we need to know that we can conquer the same things. Each road is a little bit harder for another, and each achievement plays a big part in that person’s life. How we get there matters, but why we get there is the background of it all. We do things for certain reasons, whether it’s for family or to prove to ourselves that we can do so. I thought deeply about this thought. It is bigger than us when it comes to conquering and overcoming odds because you have no idea who is watching, and having an impact on someone is a dream that everyone should have
